title: Remote: op-dropdown flashes + auto-closes on ~1s timer (models:checked rebuild storm)
description:
SYMPTOM (seen on RTX 2000 Pod during MPI-324 sweep): while remote-connected, the PromptBox operations dropdown flashes on hover and, if opened, auto-closes after a few seconds. A ~1s re-render storm. Pod logs show a per-second POST /wrapper/models/status among the telemetry GETs.

ESCALATION (2026-07-21, same sweep): the storm also CORRUPTS ACTIVE INPUT. Dragging the Denoise slider in the Krea2 settings popup, the thumb snaps back to a prior/stored position ~1x/s - the control remounts to its state value mid-drag, so the value cannot be set reliably. This is worse than the dropdown: any periodically-rebuilt control is unusable while the storm runs. Per-widget guards are therefore NOT sufficient - the fix must stop the storm at its source.

DEFECT B - the amplifier (CONFIRMED in code): MpiPromptBox.js:1657 wires Events.on('models:checked') -> _refreshOpDropdown(). _refreshOpDropdown() (MpiPromptBox.js:1194) UNCONDITIONALLY does opDropdownSlot.innerHTML='' and remounts MpiDropdown - even when the available-op set is unchanged AND even when the dropdown is open. So any models:checked tears down an open menu = flash + auto-close. The Model Library already hit this exact bug and guards it (see downloadService.js:500 comment 'models:checked -> the Model Library rebuilt every card (visible flashing)'); PromptBox never got the diff-before-rebuild guard.

DEFECT A - the trigger (HYPOTHESIS, high confidence): models:checked has ONE emitter, syncModelInstalled() (modelRegistry.js:218). It has NO frontend timer - fires only on connect/disconnect/boot/download/uninstall/slide-over open. For it to fire ~1Hz the remote:connection {connected:true} edge must be flapping while the Pod is still initializing. shell.js:1368 re-syncs on EVERY resolved connected edge by design (MPI-200 Pod-swap arch re-check) with NO debounce against repeated connected edges. Matches the user report 'says connected but it is initializing'.

SPLITTING DIAGNOSTIC - RESOLVED 2026-07-21: the storm PERSISTS at full idle (Pod IDLE/REMOTE, 9 assets done, nothing generating or downloading). Pod logs show POST /wrapper/models/status every ~2-3s plus GET /wrapper/stats ~1/s; renderer Network panel showed ~8961 total requests for the session and a SECOND repeated comfyController.js poll (URL matched 'prompt'). So this is NOT connect-flap-during-init - it is a genuine steady-state periodic poll. Note: earlier frontend grep found NO setInterval calling syncModelInstalled, so the periodic re-sync source is still not pinned - either a main-process/installStore reconciler polling /wrapper/models/status, or a renderer poll re-triggering reSyncInstalledModels, or a models:checked feedback loop. NEXT PROBE (free, do before deleting a warm Pod): Network tab filter 'check' for 10s idle - if /comfy/models/check fires ~every 2-3s the loop is renderer-side (fix at modelRegistry emit); if not, it is main-process-side.

FIX (VERIFIED against code 2026-07-21 by a root-cause trace; every hop re-confirmed by hand): ROOT = shell.js _initRemoteStatusFeed self-schedules every HEALTHY_MS=5000 (shell.js:1212-1219) and re-emits remote:connection {connected:true} on EVERY tick (shell.js:1206, comment 1189-1191) to keep the live session-cost badge climbing - NOT just on the connect edge. The _initDataRegistries listener (shell.js:1368) has no edge guard (if (connected) with no !_wasRemoteConnected), so it calls syncModelInstalled() every 5s -> remoteModelsCheck POSTs /wrapper/models/status + GETs /wrapper/manifest -> emits models:checked (modelRegistry.js:218) unconditionally. BRANCH 1 (dropdown flash): MpiPromptBox models:checked listener (1657) -> _refreshOpDropdown -> opDropdownSlot.innerHTML='' (1209). BRANCH 2 (slider snap): shell.js:1341 models:checked -> state.s_installedModelIds = ids (a fresh array each call, so the Proxy fires state:changed) -> MpiGalleryBlock onState (1653) -> setModelList (1687) -> model-unchanged branch (645) -> _refreshOpSlot (649) -> opSlot.innerHTML='' (1228) destroys the slider DOM mid-drag, rebuilt from the stored value = snap. FIX = ship BOTH: Fix A (root, shell.js:1370) edge-guard the sync so it reads if (connected AND NOT _wasRemoteConnected) - stops the every-5s syncs plus the wasted manifest + models/status polls; MPI-200 pod-swap arch re-check is preserved because a swap routes through the disconnect edge (1393) which resets _wasRemoteConnected to false; all other syncModelInstalled callers unaffected (engine:ready 1348, engineOverride 1413, boot 1424, disconnect 1393, downloadService 507/584/615/626). Fix B (defence in depth, modelRegistry.js:218) diff-gate the models:checked emit on installedModelIds+driftedModelIds so a no-change sync stays silent - zero MPI-200 risk (a pod swap changes the set so the emit still fires) and it protects all 6 consumers at once (MpiPromptBox dropdown, GalleryBlock setModelList slider, MpiAppLibrary _patchAllAffected, MpiModelManager repatch, heroStats, shell s_installedModelIds cascade). Fix A alone kills both symptoms + the wasted Pod traffic; Fix B alone kills both symptoms but leaves the 5s poll running - ship both. Deeper design smell (optional, NOT release-week): remote:connection is overloaded as both a heartbeat and a connect-edge event via the cost-badge reuse; a clean future fix gives the live specs/cost their own event so remote:connection means only an edge. The harmless /wrapper/stats 2s poll is MpiMemoryMonitor (shell.js:161) - live VRAM bar, unrelated to this bug.

SCOPE: remote engine ships in 1.2 so this is a 1.2-surface bug. Severity ESCALATED - the slider corruption makes controls unusable on remote mid-interaction, not just a cosmetic dropdown flash, which pushes toward 1.2-blocker. User chose 'keep testing, decide later' (2026-07-21); revisit at session close with the source-diff fix as the candidate. Root-cause rule: brief before patching the shared machinery.
